# 'gs commit --fill' falls back to default behavior
# when the configured generator fails.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-03-30T14:59:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git checkout -b feature
gs branch track --base main

# configure a generator that fails
git config spice.message.generator 'exit 1'

# --fill with -m still works; generator is skipped
# because -m already provides a message
git add foo.txt
gs cc --fill -m 'manual message'

git log -1 --format=%s
stdout 'manual message'

-- repo/foo.txt --
Contents of foo.
